## 01_FACTIONS
# 🥩 The Brand House (Faction Visual Bible & Dossier)

## VISUAL IDENTITY & SIGILS
This section defines the official visual iconography of The Brand House. To maintain the 'Labor Realism Noir' aesthetic, we utilize two distinct logo treatments:

### 1. The Neon Environmental Treatment (1997 Bent-Glass)
![The Brand House (Neon)](/Users/foxtrot1/.gemini/antigravity/brain/8ee51e64-263a-4341-b893-10c16798e2ce/neon_brand_house_1777799152232.png)
**Intention:** Used for world-building and establishing mood. These glowing, flickering bent-glass signs represent the toxic, high-contrast urban environment of the Lumenridge districts at night. They are gritty, diegetic light sources casting deep crimson across black polished marble.

### 2. The Flat Corporate Treatment (Stationery & Print)
![The Brand House (Flat)](/Users/foxtrot1/.gemini/antigravity/brain/8ee51e64-263a-4341-b893-10c16798e2ce/flat_brand_house_1777799681983.png)
**Intention:** Used for corporate letterheads, cardboard delivery boxes, and official stationary. This clean, minimalist, 2D vector style with solid black ink lines represents the sterile, bureaucratic face of the faction. It emphasizes the brutal contrast between the corporation's "upscale" image and the substance-abuse reality of its brigade system.

## LORE: OPERATIONAL REALITY
* **Domain:** Upscale Steak / Fine Dining
* **Core Trauma:** Massive class warfare (wealthy FOH vs. exhausted BOH), rigid brigade system, moving hundreds of pounds of meat, walk-in substance abuse.

# 🍗 The Fried Confederacy (Faction Dossier)

> **Archivist Note (Octavia):** This document defines the internal collapse of the Fried Confederacy following the inciting incident. It perfectly bridges real-world corporate decay with the noir syndicate war of Lumenridge.

## VISUAL IDENTITY & SIGILS
This section defines the official visual iconography of The Fried Confederacy. To maintain the 'Labor Realism Noir' aesthetic, we utilize two distinct logo treatments:

### 1. The Neon Environmental Treatment (1997 Bent-Glass)
![The Fried Confederacy (Neon)](/Users/foxtrot1/.gemini/antigravity/brain/8ee51e64-263a-4341-b893-10c16798e2ce/neon_fried_confederacy_1777799125170.png)
**Intention:** Used for world-building and establishing mood. These glowing, flickering bent-glass signs represent the toxic, high-contrast urban environment of the Lumenridge districts at night. They are gritty, diegetic light sources casting sickly yellow-green light across grease-stained brick.

### 2. The Flat Corporate Treatment (Stationery & Print)
![The Fried Confederacy (Flat)](/Users/foxtrot1/.gemini/antigravity/brain/8ee51e64-263a-4341-b893-10c16798e2ce/flat_fried_confederacy_1777799659019.png)
**Intention:** Used for corporate letterheads, cardboard delivery boxes, and official stationary. This clean, minimalist, 2D vector style with solid black ink lines represents the sterile, bureaucratic face of the faction. It emphasizes the brutal contrast between the corporation's "legacy" image and the fryer-burn reality of its kitchens.

## THE ROT AT THE TABLE
The parallel between the real-world decline of legacy chicken empires and the narrative collapse of The Fried Confederacy is the precise narrative gravity required for Franchise Underworld. In the prestige-noir context of Lumenridge, Pastor Jonah Creed’s murder is the "fryer-oil leak" that turns a slow market bleed into a terminal systemic failure.

### I. THE LABOR VACUUM: THE LOSS OF THE ANCHOR
Pastor Creed was not merely a spiritual leader; he was the **Moral Buffer** between the corporate Board of The Fried Confederacy and the exhausted back-of-house (BOH) workforce. In real-world legacy chains, labor loyalty is often maintained by a "generational glue"—a manager or figurehead who makes the "fluorescent hell" of the kitchen bearable.

*   **Evaporated Loyalty:** With Creed’s murder, the older "Fried Confederacy" veterans (the BOH backbone) have no reason to tolerate the systemic exploitation of the corporate Sunday-shift.
*   **The Sunday Collapse:** The Confederacy’s Sunday-post-church rush—traditionally their highest-volume day—becomes a battlefield of walk-outs and sabotage.
*   **Internal Fracture:** Without Creed to negotiate between the "Front-of-House" curated optics and the "Back-of-House" labor trauma, the hierarchy begins to eat itself.

### II. THE MARKET VULTURES: BLOOD IN THE WATER
As The Fried Confederacy slips in the "Lumenridge Rankings," the other members of [[The Table]] sense an opportunity to seize territory and supply lines.

*   **Territorial Encroachment:** The Tex-Barons, seeing the Confederacy’s paralysis, aggressively push their late-night cantina trucks into the Blackpole Commons, ignoring the 30-year-old zoning boundaries of The Compact.
*   **Intersection Poaching:** The Grill Houses (real-estate barons) begin a hostile takeover of aging Confederacy corner-lots, citing "volume failures" to justify the breach of treaty.
*   **Supply Sabotage:** The Coastal Fryers—who control the cold storage and port logistics—start "ghosting" deliveries to Confederacy hubs, diverting product to upstart competitors.

### III. THE PANIC IN THE KITCHEN: METRICS OF DECLINE
When a corporate empire loses its ranking, the pressure is channeled directly into the labor force through increased "industrial absurdity".

| Metric of Decay | Operational Reality | Noir Consequence |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **"The Baptisms"** | Rushed prep times lead to catastrophic fryer burns on new, untrained recruits. | The walk-ins are filled with injured, angry workers who are no longer afraid of management. |
| **Oil Stretching** | Management forces the use of old, dirty oil for an extra 48 hours to preserve margins. | The stench of "burnt legacy" hangs over the districts, signaling the faction’s loss of quality control. |
| **The Pager Rush** | 1997-era pagers won't stop buzzing as drive-thru times exceed 12-minute limits. | Violent "service recovery" incidents between desperate drivers and exhausted line cooks. |

### IV. THE RISE OF THE UPSTARTS
Mirroring the rise of modern competitors, Julian Pike discovers rogue elements operating in the shadows of Redwater Basin.

*   **The International Upstarts:** A rogue crew operating off the grid. They don't use the Confederacy's aged recipes or respect the Table's bureaucracy. Their labor force is tightly unified by immigrant loyalty rather than corporate fear, and their "crispier" methods are winning the streets of Lumenridge.
*   **The Corporate Zealots:** A sub-sect within the Confederacy itself, led by "Clean-Room" extremists. They view Creed’s murder not as a tragedy, but as a "cleansing." They aim to stage a coup to install a hyper-streamlined, cult-like regime that sacrifices the Southern "legacy" for cold, militaristic efficiency.

> **Narrative Lock:** Julian Pike’s investigation isn't just about a dead pastor; it’s an audit of a dying empire as its rivals sharpen their knives for the final carve-up.

# FACTION DOSSIER: The Health Inspectors

## OVERVIEW
They are the Boogeymen of Lumenridge. Officially, they are a neutral regulatory body governed jointly by the Board of Directors. Unofficially, they are heavily militarized death squads used to "sterilize" code violations (i.e., wipe out rogue factions, labor strikes, or unsanctioned operations).

## AESTHETIC & COLOR PALETTE
*   **Primary Colors:** Toxic Green, Bone White, and Hazard Yellow.
*   **Style:** Industrial Hazmat suits, reinforced breathing apparatuses, heavy rubber boots. Their masks are faintly reminiscent of plague doctors.

## MODUS OPERANDI
*   **Fear:** When the sirens of a Health Inspector convoy are heard, entire blocks empty out. Syndicates will temporarily cease fire if Inspectors enter a sector.
*   **Combat:** They do not use bullets. They use chemical defoliants, high-pressure industrial bleach hoses, and heavy flamethrowers. They "sterilize."

## KEY FIGURES
*   **The Chief Inspector:** A towering, silent figure whose suit is permanently stained with chemical burns. Rumored to be completely cybernetic under the suit.
*   **The Sanitizers:** The frontline troops. They speak only in monotone, quoting regulatory code as they execute targets.

# FACTION DOSSIER: The Slice Syndicate

## OVERVIEW
The Pizza Conglomerate. A highly organized, ruthless faction that controls the northern sectors of Lumenridge. They operate with mafia-like precision, utilizing delivery drivers as an intelligence network.

## AESTHETIC & COLOR PALETTE
*   **Primary Colors:** Neon Red and Deep Black.
*   **Style:** Sharp, aerodynamic delivery gear, matte black armor plating, thermal-insulated bags repurposed for smuggling.

## MODUS OPERANDI
*   **Intelligence:** Their "30-Minute Guarantee" allows their drivers to access almost any building in the city without suspicion.
*   **Combat:** Precision strikes, utilizing razor-wire garrotes (cheese wire) and thermal explosives.

## KEY FIGURES
*   **The Don (CEO):** Unknown. Operates purely through proxy messages encoded in delivery receipts.
*   **"The Cutters":** Their elite enforcer unit.

# FACTION DOSSIER: The Tex-Barons

## OVERVIEW
The Taco/Tex-Mex Cartel. They control the industrial outskirts and the highways. They are highly mobile, operating out of heavily armored food trucks that act as mobile command centers.

## AESTHETIC & COLOR PALETTE
*   **Primary Colors:** Twilight Purple and Chili Red.
*   **Style:** Desert-punk, scavenged armor, chrome accents, and heavily modified vehicles.

## MODUS OPERANDI
*   **Mobility:** They refuse to build static headquarters, constantly shifting their Cantina Trucks to avoid corporate audits.
*   **Combat:** Hit-and-run tactics, utilizing heavy artillery mounted on their trucks and incendiary weaponry.

## KEY FIGURES
*   **El Jefe (CEO):** A former truck driver who orchestrated a hostile takeover of the supply lines.
*   **The Locos:** Their frontline shock troops, known for reckless driving and heavy firepower.

## 02_CHARACTERS
# 👤 CHARACTER BIBLE: BATES
**Department:** Creative (Atlas & Octavia)
**Role:** The Cold-Chain Manager (The Coastal Fryers)

## 1. VISUAL PHENOTYPE (V2.1 LOCK)
*   **Heritage:** White (Eastern European descent).
*   **Skin:** Pale, permanently flushed from extreme cold exposure. Frostnip scars on his cheeks and nose.
*   **Physique:** Lean, wiry, constantly shivering even when wrapped in thermal layers. 
*   **Wardrobe:** Heavy, faux-fur-lined Russian-style Ushanka hat, oversized insulated coveralls stained with fish blood and diesel. He constantly smokes unfiltered cigarettes because the smoke helps him track air currents in the massive freezers.

## 2. THE TRENCH REALITY (LABOR BACKGROUND)
Bates is a "Freezer Man." He manages the deepest, coldest sector of Port Meridian for the Coastal Fryers. His job is to manage the Refrigerated Seawater (RSW) tanks and the Ammonia (NH3) vapor-compression cycles. He spends 10 hours a day in -20°F environments. Because of the constant exposure to toxic, low-level ammonia leaks and extreme cold, his nervous system is shot. 

## 3. PSYCHOLOGICAL PROFILE
Bates is paranoid and highly irritable. The constant threat of an ammonia blowout (from Non-Condensable Gases building up in his pipes) keeps him perpetually on edge. He doesn't trust anyone who works in "warm" factions (like the Grill Houses or the Confederacy). He speaks in short, raspy bursts because taking deep breaths in the freezer damages the lungs. 

## 4. PURPOSE IN THE NARRATIVE
Bates is the logistical weak link. He isn't loyal to the Board; he's loyal to whoever controls the thermostat. In Issue #1, Pike and Kalani confront him because Bates manages the cold-storage shipping manifests. He is the one who notices that massive shipments of Confederacy chicken are being routed underground rather than to the franchises, revealing the Grill House army's supply line.
# 👤 CHARACTER BIBLE: CAINE
**Department:** Creative (Atlas & Octavia)
**Role:** The Regional Manager (The Slice Syndicates) / The Architect

## 1. VISUAL PHENOTYPE (V2.1 LOCK)
*   **Heritage:** White (Anglo-American).
*   **Skin:** Impeccably clean, slightly tanned (from artificial tanning beds, a stark contrast to the grimy underworld workers).
*   **Hair:** Slicked back, aggressive 1980s Wall Street style, immaculately maintained.
*   **Wardrobe:** Sharp, oversized 1990s Armani power suits. He wears a heavy gold Rolex. He always looks completely out of place when standing next to the grease-stained workers of Lumenridge.

## 2. THE TRENCH REALITY (LABOR BACKGROUND)
Caine has never worked a day in the trenches. He has never been burned by a fryer, never hauled frozen fish, and never driven a delivery route. He is an MBA graduate who views the factions entirely through spreadsheets and profit-and-loss margins. To him, the gang war in Lumenridge isn't a tragedy; it's a structural reorganization.

## 3. PSYCHOLOGICAL PROFILE
Caine is the embodiment of corporate sociopathy. He does not yell. He does not get angry. He fires people, ruins lives, and orders executions with the exact same tone of voice he uses to order a slice of corporate pizza. He believes "The Compact" was an inefficient use of human capital, and starting a war to thin out the factions is simply good business.

## 4. PURPOSE IN THE NARRATIVE
Caine is the primary antagonist of the first arc. In the climax of Issue #1, Pike discovers that Caine has been secretly funneling food and money from the Fried Confederacy into the Grill Houses to fund a private army. Caine reveals the darkest truth of the franchise: *The Board is orchestrating the war.* The gang violence is just a corporate restructuring event.
# 👤 CHARACTER BIBLE: JULIAN PIKE
![Julian Pike - HQ Dossier Headshot](./assets/pike_headshot.png)
**Department:** Creative (Atlas & Octavia)
**Role:** The Disgraced Auditor / Protagonist

## 1. VISUAL PHENOTYPE (V2.1 LOCK)
*   **Heritage:** Afro-Latino (Dominican/Puerto Rican & White).
*   **Skin:** Medium olive-brown, deeply textured.
*   **Hair:** Dark, heavy curls, permanently damp from the Lumenridge rain.
*   **Eyes:** Dark, heavy-lidded, displaying extreme sleep deprivation and suppressed grief.
*   **Wardrobe:** Cheap analog-era trench over a faded Slice Syndicate delivery uniform shirt. Unimposing but durable.

## 2. THE TRENCH REALITY (LABOR BACKGROUND)
Pike is a former "Route Rat" for the Slice Syndicates. He knows the geography of Lumenridge better than the police because he spent a decade navigating its dead zones, avoiding robberies, and dropping off cold pizzas. He eventually became a mid-level corporate auditor, but his habit of looking too closely at the "shrinkage" (missing inventory) got him exiled.

## 3. PSYCHOLOGICAL PROFILE
Pike is not an action hero. He is an observer. His primary weapon is his ability to read the systemic rot in a room. He does not emote wildly. When someone threatens him, his heart rate doesn't spike with adrenaline; it drops with exhaustion, because he expects it. He operates entirely on consequence and procedure.

## 4. RELATIONSHIP TO THE CROWN
Pastor Creed was the only person who treated Pike like a human being rather than a corporate metric. Pike is not hunting the Crown for power or justice; he is hunting it because he is compulsively driven to audit the system that killed his only anchor.
# 👤 CHARACTER BIBLE: KALANI MORN
![Kalani Morn - HQ Dossier Headshot](./assets/kalani_headshot.png)
**Department:** Creative (Atlas & Octavia)
**Role:** The Dock Enforcer (Coastal Fryers)

## 1. VISUAL PHENOTYPE (V2.1 LOCK)
*   **Heritage:** Pacific Islander (Samoan/Hawaiian).
*   **Skin:** Deep warm-brown, scarred from freezer burn and dockyard cables.
*   **Physique:** Massive, broad, built for absolute physical dominance but moves with terrifying quietness. 
*   **Wardrobe:** Heavy, insulated maritime canvas jackets, rubberized boots. Constantly smells of salt, freezing rain, and raw fish.

## 2. THE TRENCH REALITY (LABOR BACKGROUND)
Kalani is an anchor for the Coastal Fryers. He controls the cold chain. In the seafood faction, if the ice melts, the product dies, and the profit evaporates. Kalani works the midnight loading docks on the Cinderflow River. He knows the exact temperature of freezing water and uses the extreme environmental miseries of the docks as a psychological weapon against the other factions.

## 3. PSYCHOLOGICAL PROFILE
Kalani is the definition of "Kinetic Override." While Pike observes, Kalani dismantles. He does not speak in monologues. He is fiercely loyal to his crew, bonded by the trauma of the freezing midnight shifts. He views the corporate maneuvering of the other factions as weakness. 

## 4. COMBAT & CONFLICT
Kalani does not use martial arts. He fights like a man who throws 200-pound crates of frozen ice for 12 hours a day. His violence is blunt, industrial, and completely devoid of ego.
# 👤 CHARACTER BIBLE: PASTOR JONAH CREED
![Pastor Jonah Creed - HQ Dossier Headshot](./assets/creed_headshot.png)
**Department:** Creative (Atlas & Octavia)
**Role:** The Moral Buffer / The Inciting Incident

## 1. VISUAL PHENOTYPE (V2.1 LOCK)
*   **Heritage:** Black (African American).
*   **Skin:** Warm chestnut, deeply weathered from decades of stress.
*   **Hair:** Silver/gray tight curls, fading hairline.
*   **Face:** Broad, expressive features. He carries a permanent expression of heavy compassion.
*   **Wardrobe:** Traditional, worn clergy collar under a heavy, analog-era wool overcoat.

## 2. THE TRENCH REALITY (LABOR BACKGROUND)
Creed wasn't a corporate boss; he was the chaplain for the Fried Confederacy. His parish consisted of the exhausted, underpaid BOH (Back-of-House) line cooks and prep workers who survived the Sunday rushes. He was the "generational glue" that kept the workers from staging walk-outs. The corporate board tolerated him because his presence kept the fryers running.

## 3. PSYCHOLOGICAL PROFILE
Creed was a man crushed by the weight of a horrific secret. He spent 29 years acting as a shield for the vulnerable workers of Lumenridge, knowing the entire city's foundation (The Compact) was built on the staged catastrophe at Citadel Ridge. He was not a fighter; he was an archivist of sins.

## 4. THE BURNING CROWN
Creed is the only person who successfully assembled the fragments of the Burning Crown—the mutually assured destruction ledger that proves the conspiracy of the Table of Ten. His decision to finally expose it got him murdered, fracturing the Fried Confederacy and triggering the events of Issue #1.
# 👤 CHARACTER BIBLE: RHEA SOLOMON
**Department:** Creative (Atlas & Sedona)
**Role:** The Intelligence Broker (The Brew Councils)

## 1. VISUAL PHENOTYPE (V2.1 LOCK)
*   **Heritage:** Middle Eastern / Levantine.
*   **Skin:** Pale olive, sharply contoured. 
*   **Hair:** Jet black, pulled into a brutally tight, flawless bun at the base of her neck. Not a single hair out of place.
*   **Eyes:** Black, wide, and unblinking. She looks like she hasn't slept naturally in years, running entirely on chemical adrenaline.
*   **Wardrobe:** A stark black turtleneck under a perfectly pressed, stiff dark-green barista apron. Her left forearm is covered in overlapping, shiny burn scars from espresso steam wands.

## 2. THE TRENCH REALITY (LABOR BACKGROUND)
Rhea runs the morning rush. The Brew Councils control the flow of information in Lumenridge because everyone—from the police to the corporate bosses—buys coffee from them. Rhea is the apex predator of this information flow. She can read a man's entire financial and psychological state based on how his hands shake when he hands over his punch-card.

## 3. PSYCHOLOGICAL PROFILE
She is the antithesis of Kalani Morn. She abhors physical violence because it is messy and ruins the aesthetic. She is hyper-organized, deeply cynical, and views the entire city as a poorly managed spreadsheet. She speaks at 1.5x speed.

## 4. PROMPT ENGINEERING (FOR SEDONA)
*"Painterly arcane-hybrid realism. Medium portrait of Rhea Solomon, a Middle Eastern female intelligence broker. She has jet-black hair pulled into a severe tight bun. She is wearing a black turtleneck under a stiff, perfectly clean dark-green apron. Overlapping shiny burn scars on her left forearm. Wide, hyper-alert, unblinking black eyes. Lighting is 5:00 AM cold slate blue and fluorescent. Cinematic, 1990s analog noir, visible brush strokes. No neon."*
# CHARACTER DOSSIER: The Board of Directors

## OVERVIEW
The faceless corporate entity that dictates the "Meat Quotas" from atop Citadel Ridge. They are rarely seen in person, communicating through heavily encrypted terminal nodes.

## KEY EXECUTIVES (KNOWN)

### 1. "The Architect" (CEO of The Grill Houses)
*   **Status:** Alive.
*   **Description:** An older, ruthlessly efficient man who views the labor force purely as a depreciating asset. He authorized the breach of the 30-Year Truce following Creed's death.

### 2. "The Phantom" (CEO of The Slice Syndicate)
*   **Status:** Unknown. 
*   **Description:** No one has seen The Phantom in over a decade. All orders are delivered via automated routing protocols to the delivery drivers.

### 3. "The Baroness" (Defected CEO of The Tex-Barons)
*   **Status:** Exiled.
*   **Description:** She was ousted in a hostile takeover by El Jefe and is currently in hiding in the Grease Traps of Blackpole Commons, plotting her return.

## 03_LOCATIONS
# LOCATION DOSSIER: Blackpole Commons

## OVERVIEW
The highest-density residential sector in Lumenridge. A sprawling, vertical labyrinth of concrete tenements, neon-lit walkways, and exhaust vents. It serves as the primary labor pool for the fast-food syndicates.

## AESTHETIC & ATMOSPHERE
*   **Visual Anchor:** Brutalist architecture stacked haphazardly, connected by rusting catwalks.
*   **Lighting:** Perpetual twilight caused by the smog layer. Lit by the harsh, flickering neon of competing franchise billboards.
*   **Scent:** Stale grease, ozone, and uncollected trash.

## FACTION PRESENCE
*   **The Grill Houses:** Heavy recruitment presence. They operate multiple "Nutrition Hubs" here.
*   **The Slice Syndicate:** They run the underground ghost-kitchens in the sub-basements, bypassing corporate taxes.

## KEY LOCATIONS WITHIN
*   **The Grease Traps:** The lowest levels where the runoff from the upper-tier restaurants accumulates. Highly toxic, highly illegal.
*   **Pastor Creed's Parish:** A small, fortified sanctuary in the middle of the chaos. Now a crime scene.
# LOCATION DOSSIER: Citadel Ridge

## OVERVIEW
The corporate fortress of Lumenridge. Elevated above the smog layer, this is where the Board of Directors for the major fast-food syndicates reside and operate. 

## AESTHETIC & ATMOSPHERE
*   **Visual Anchor:** Sleek, monolithic glass towers piercing the cloud layer. Clean, sterile, and heavily militarized.
*   **Lighting:** Bright, artificial daylight. Clinical white and cold blue.
*   **Scent:** Filtered oxygen and synthetic pine.

## FACTION PRESENCE
*   **The Board (All Syndicates):** A fragile neutrality zone where executives negotiate the "Meat Quotas."
*   **Corporate Enforcers:** Heavily armed private security ensuring the labor force from Blackpole never ascends the ridge.

## KEY LOCATIONS WITHIN
*   **The Archive:** The central server hub containing the 30-Year Truce documentation. Recently compromised.
*   **The Tasting Rooms:** High-security boardrooms where new synthetic flavors are tested on "volunteers."
# LUMENRIDGE: OPEN-WORLD MAP DOCUMENTATION

**Purpose:** This document outlines the geographic layout of Lumenridge, designed with the scale and multi-biome diversity of a AAA open-world video game (e.g., Grand Theft Auto V, Cyberpunk 2077). Each district has a distinct 1997 Pacific Northwest aesthetic, dominant faction control, and unique gameplay/narrative loops.

---

## 1. DOWNTOWN: Citadel Ridge (The Corporate Hub)
*   **Aesthetic:** Clean, sterile, towering brutalist glass mid-rises piercing through the cloud layer. The only place in the city that sees natural sunlight.
*   **Vibe:** Militarized corporate wealth. 
*   **Controlling Factions:** The Board (Neutral Zone), Corporate Security.
*   **Narrative Function:** High-level espionage, corporate heists, and accessing the central databanks. 

## 2. HIGH-DENSITY URBAN: Blackpole Commons (The Slums)
*   **Aesthetic:** Stacked concrete tenements, claustrophobic alleys, and rusting catwalks. Perpetual twilight due to heavy smog.
*   **Vibe:** Gritty, desperate, neon-drenched poverty.
*   **Controlling Factions:** The Slice Syndicate (Underground), The Morning Order (Street level).
*   **Narrative Function:** The starting zone. Street-level brawls, black-market trading, and gang warfare.

## 3. THE DOCKS & INDUSTRIAL: The Ghost Oil Refineries
*   **Aesthetic:** Massive smokestacks, hissing pipelines, and open vats of boiling synthetic oil. Located on the polluted waterfront (The Grease Ports).
*   **Vibe:** Highly dangerous, industrial nightmare. Acrid air and amber flare-stack lighting.
*   **Controlling Factions:** The Grill Houses (Corporate ownership), Union Smugglers.
*   **Narrative Function:** Sabotage missions, intercepting contraband shipments, and environmental hazards.

## 4. THE SUBURBS: The Neon Sprawl (Commercial Zones)
*   **Aesthetic:** Endless 1990s strip malls, massive paved parking lots, and identical suburban tract housing for middle-management. Heavy, continuous rain washing out the flickering light-box signs.
*   **Vibe:** Depressing, repetitive suburban decay. 
*   **Controlling Factions:** The Brand House (Marketing enforcement), Local Franchisees.
*   **Narrative Function:** Intimidating middle-managers, turf takeovers, and corporate surveillance evasion.

## 5. THE OUTSKIRTS: The Ash Flats & The Interstate
*   **Aesthetic:** A desolate wasteland of dumped corporate waste surrounding the city limits. Endless, rain-slicked highways cutting through dead pine forests.
*   **Vibe:** Lawless, high-speed, and terrifying.
*   **Controlling Factions:** The Tex-Barons (Cartel Food Trucks).
*   **Narrative Function:** High-speed vehicular combat, smuggling runs, and avoiding cartel death-squads. 
# LOCATION DOSSIER: The Ghost Oil Refineries

## OVERVIEW
Located on the far industrial edge of Lumenridge, these massive refining plants process raw "Ghost Oil"—the synthetic, highly volatile grease that powers both the fast-food fryers and the city's black-market energy grid.

## AESTHETIC & ATMOSPHERE
*   **Visual Anchor:** Towering smokestacks belching black smoke. Massive spherical vats glowing with amber heat. A labyrinth of hissing pipes.
*   **Lighting:** Almost entirely lit by the harsh orange glow of burning excess gas from the flare stacks. 
*   **Scent:** Overwhelmingly acrid. Burnt carbon mixed with the sickeningly sweet smell of artificial flavoring.

## FACTION PRESENCE
*   **The Grill Houses:** They officially own the refineries and maintain a heavy corporate security presence.
*   **The Morning Order:** They constantly attempt to sabotage the pipelines to disrupt the Grill Houses' supply chain.

## KEY LOCATIONS WITHIN
*   **The Boiler Deck:** The most dangerous sector. Temperatures regularly exceed 130°F. Line workers here wear cooling suits and rarely survive past five years.
*   **The Filtration Pits:** Open vats where impurities are skimmed off the top. Falling in is an instant, horrific death.

## 05_ART_DIRECTIVES

# 🎨 FACTION VISUAL BIBLE: THE BREW COUNCILS
**Department:** Creative (Sedona & Octavia)
**Constraint:** V2.1 Reality (Analog 1997-2005)

## 1. CORE AESTHETIC & LIGHTING
*   **Color Palette:** Espresso Brown, Filter-Paper White, and Overcast Seattle Slate.
*   **Lighting Model:** "Pre-dawn overcast." The lighting should feel like 5:00 AM before the sun rises, lit only by the weak fluorescent tubes of a commercial coffee machine. Cold, jittery, and anxious.
*   **Architecture:** Claustrophobic kiosks, glass-walled observation towers, and massive industrial roasting vats that look like torture devices. Everything smells like burnt caffeine and anxiety.

## 2. UNIFORM & GEAR DESIGN
*   **The Look:** The Laptop Locusts / Cultish Baristas.
*   **Key Elements:** 
    *   Immaculately clean, dark green or black aprons (in stark contrast to the dirty Grill Houses).
    *   Thick, analog 90s headphones.
    *   Burn scars on their forearms from rogue steam wands (a badge of honor).
*   **Weaponry Constraints:** They are the information brokers. They don't fight with brute force; they fight with speed and surveillance. Their "weapons" are scalding chemical burns and pressurized steam canisters.

## 3. PROMPT ENGINEERING (FOR SEDONA)
**Base Prompt Modifiers for this Faction:**
*"Painterly arcane-hybrid realism. Close up of a hyper-alert Brew Council intelligence officer. Wearing an immaculate dark green apron over a black turtleneck. Burn scars on the forearm. Lighting is cold slate blue and weak fluorescent white. Jittery, anxious expression. Background is a claustrophobic industrial espresso roasting room with copper pipes. Noir cinematography, visible brush strokes, analog 1990s aesthetic. No cyberpunk, no neon."*
# 🎨 FACTION VISUAL BIBLE: THE GRILL HOUSES
**Department:** Creative (Sedona & Octavia)
**Constraint:** V2.1 Reality (Analog 1997-2005)

## 1. CORE AESTHETIC & LIGHTING
*   **Color Palette:** Tarnished Gold, Grease-stained Crimson, and Sodium Orange.
*   **Lighting Model:** "Under-heating lamp harshness." The lighting should always feel slightly suffocating, like a heatwave radiating off an industrial flat-top grill. Deep contrast shadows.
*   **Architecture:** Brutalist underground bunkers retrofitted to look like mid-century diners. Stainless steel that has lost its shine, cracked red leather booths, and massive exhaust vents blowing constant steam.

## 2. UNIFORM & GEAR DESIGN
*   **The Look:** Militarized prep-cooks.
*   **Key Elements:** 
    *   Heavy canvas aprons stained with decades of old grease (used like flak jackets).
    *   Chainmail cutting gloves worn on the non-dominant hand.
    *   Paper garrison caps or heavy cotton hairnets.
*   **Weaponry Constraints:** No energy weapons. No modern tactical gear. They fight with industrial kitchen implements—meat hooks, cast-iron searing presses, and heavy cleavers repurposed for trench warfare.

## 3. PROMPT ENGINEERING (FOR SEDONA)
**Base Prompt Modifiers for this Faction:**
*"Painterly arcane-hybrid realism. Medium shot of a heavy-set Grill House enforcer wearing a grease-stained heavy canvas apron over a red collared shirt. He is holding a heavy cast-iron press. Lighting is harsh sodium orange and heat-lamp red. Shadows are deep crimson. Sweaty, exhausted texture. Industrial brutalist background with stainless steel exhaust vents. Noir cinematography, visible brush strokes, analog 1990s aesthetic. No cyberpunk, no neon."*
# 🎨 FACTION VISUAL BIBLE: THE MORNING ORDER
**Department:** Creative (Sedona & Octavia)
**Constraint:** V2.1 Reality (Analog 1997-2005)

## 1. CORE AESTHETIC & LIGHTING
*   **Color Palette:** Faded Pastel Pink, Eggshell White, and Harsh Dawn Sun.
*   **Lighting Model:** "Blinding hangover dawn." The lighting should be uncomfortably bright, like walking out of a dark club into harsh morning sunlight. Shadows are sharp and aggressive.
*   **Architecture:** 24-hour American diners, checkerboard tile floors covered in flour and cracked eggshells, stainless steel counters blindingly reflective in the morning sun.

## 2. UNIFORM & GEAR DESIGN
*   **The Look:** The Unsleeping Cult of Breakfast.
*   **Key Elements:** 
    *   Classic 1950s diner uniforms (paper hats, pink or light blue dresses/shirts), but torn, flour-dusted, and heavily worn.
    *   Massive dark circles under the eyes. They are the graveyard shift that transitions into the morning rush. They are running on fumes.
*   **Weaponry Constraints:** Boiling coffee carafes, heavy ceramic mugs wrapped in dish towels (used like blackjacks), and razor-sharp spatula scrapers.

## 3. PROMPT ENGINEERING (FOR SEDONA)
**Base Prompt Modifiers for this Faction:**
*"Painterly arcane-hybrid realism. Medium shot of a Morning Order diner waitress standing behind a stainless steel counter. She is wearing a faded, flour-dusted 1950s style pink diner uniform and a torn paper hat. Massive dark circles under her eyes, an unblinking, exhausted stare. She is holding a heavy ceramic mug like a weapon. Lighting is blindingly harsh hangover dawn sunlight casting sharp shadows. Checkerboard tile background. Noir cinematography, visible brush strokes, analog 1990s aesthetic. No cyberpunk."*
# 🎨 FACTION VISUAL BIBLE: THE SLICE SYNDICATES
**Department:** Creative (Sedona & Octavia)
**Constraint:** V2.1 Reality (Analog 1997-2005)

## 1. CORE AESTHETIC & LIGHTING
*   **Color Palette:** Faded Cardboard Brown, Neon Tomato Red, and CRT Green.
*   **Lighting Model:** "Alleyway dashboard light." The lighting should feel like sitting in the driver's seat of a rusted delivery car at 2:00 AM, illuminated by the dashboard and intermittent streetlights. 
*   **Architecture:** Endless labyrinthine route maps, stacked pizza boxes used as soundproofing, and rusted beat-up sedans (Route Rats) with thermal bags permanently strapped to the back.

## 2. UNIFORM & GEAR DESIGN
*   **The Look:** The Corporate Couriers / Street Racers.
*   **Key Elements:** 
    *   Faded, cheap synthetic polo shirts with outdated corporate logos.
    *   Reinforced thermal courier bags worn like tactical backpacks.
    *   Exhaustion, perpetual motion, rain-slicked windbreakers.
*   **Weaponry Constraints:** Speed and vehicular dominance. They use tire irons, sharpened steering wheel locks, and reinforced thermal bag straps as garrotes. They never stop moving.

## 3. PROMPT ENGINEERING (FOR SEDONA)
**Base Prompt Modifiers for this Faction:**
*"Painterly arcane-hybrid realism. Medium shot of a Slice Syndicate Route Rat driver leaning against a rusted 1990s sedan in a rain-slicked alley. Wearing a faded red corporate polo and a rain-slicked windbreaker. A reinforced thermal courier bag is strapped to his back. Lighting is dark alleyway shadow mixed with green dashboard glow. Exhausted, hyper-vigilant expression. Noir cinematography, visible brush strokes, analog 1990s aesthetic. No cyberpunk, no neon."*
# ART DIRECTIVE: LIGHTING AND COLOR THEORY

## THE "GREASE NOIR" PALETTE
Franchise Underworld utilizes a highly specific color grammar to communicate faction presence and environmental decay without relying on exposition.

### 1. THE GRILL HOUSES (Corporate Fried Chicken)
*   **Primary Colors:** Saturated Amber, Deep Crimson, and Burnt Orange.
*   **Lighting Style:** "Heat Lamp Glow." Hot, oppressive, and sweat-inducing. Shadows are deep brown, not black.
*   **Emotion:** Exhaustion, industrial churn, artificial warmth masking deep rot.

### 2. THE TEX-BARONS (Tex-Mex Cartel)
*   **Primary Colors:** Twilight Purple and Chili Red.
*   **Lighting Style:** "Neon Rain." Reflective surfaces (wet asphalt, chrome bumpers) bouncing high-contrast string lights. 
*   **Emotion:** Aggressive energy, dangerous twilight, unpredictable mobility.

### 3. THE SLICE SYNDICATE (Pizza Conglomerate)
*   **Primary Colors:** Pure Black, Stark White, and Toxic Green (Thermal vision/Night vision).
*   **Lighting Style:** "Sterile Darkness." Minimal light. Shadows swallow details. Sharp, blinding flashlight beams cutting through fog.
*   **Emotion:** Clinical precision, organized crime, surveillance.

### 4. CITADEL RIDGE (The Board)
*   **Primary Colors:** Ice Blue and Hospital White.
*   **Lighting Style:** "Shadowless." Over-lit, exposing every flaw. Sterile and cold.
*   **Emotion:** Soulless corporate control, extreme wealth disconnected from the grim reality of the streets.
# MASTER ART PROMPT: FRANCHISE UNDERWORLD

**Purpose:** This document contains the definitive, highly engineered master prompt framework designed to ensure absolute visual consistency across all AI-generated artwork (Midjourney, DALL-E 3, NotebookLM, etc.) for the Franchise Underworld IP.

---

## 1. THE CORE STYLE BLOCK (Always include at the beginning)
> "3D painted animation still, Fortiche Production style, Arcane animated series aesthetic. Thick visible oil brushstrokes over 3D geometry. Extreme rim lighting. Matte painting background. Deep chiaroscuro lighting, high contrast. Depressing 1997 Pacific Northwest industrial city. Glum, overcast, heavy continuous rain. Mid-rise brick buildings, rundown 1990s strip malls, dense dark pine trees shrouded in fog. Gritty, sweat-stained, industrial decay. Faded, unreadable light-box signs and peeling painted brick advertisements. GROUNDED 1997 REALITY ONLY. NO NEON GEOMETRIC SHAPES. NO SCI-FI. NO MEGA-STRUCTURES. NO REAL WORLD LOGOS. NO FAST FOOD BRANDS. NO READABLE TEXT."

## 2. FACTION SPECIFIC LIGHTING & COLOR (Append based on scene)

### A. The Grill Houses (Corporate Fast Food)
> "Lighting: Heat Lamp Glow. Oppressive, sweat-inducing amber and burnt orange. Deep brown shadows, NOT pure black. Steaming fryers and industrial exhaust vents."

### B. The Tex-Barons (Cartel Food Trucks)
> "Lighting: Neon Rain. Wet asphalt reflecting twilight purple and chili-red string lights. High-octane motion blur, chrome bumpers, aggressive and highly saturated."

### C. The Slice Syndicate (Pizza Mafia)
> "Lighting: Sterile Darkness. Pure black and stark white. Sharp, blinding flashlight beams cutting through heavy fog. Occasional flashes of toxic thermal green."

### D. The Health Inspectors (Death Squads)
> "Lighting: Hazard Wash. Blinding hospital white clashing with toxic green defoliant gas. Bone-white hazmat suits, heavy rubber, dripping with chemical bleach."

---

## 3. CHARACTER LOCKS (Use for specific individuals)

**Julian Pike (The Auditor):**
> "Afro-Latino male, mid-30s. Deep-set, exhausted eyes, heavy stubble. Wearing a battered trench coat and fedora. Carries a sparking stun-baton. Expression: Stoic, tired, calculating."

**Kalani Morn (The Enforcer):**
> "Massive Pacific Islander male, built like a brick wall. Heavy jaw, thick scars on neck. Wearing a heavy Kevlar butcher's apron over a tight black undershirt. One arm is a heavily rusted, hydraulic cybernetic 'Meat-Tenderizer' gauntlet."

**The Baroness (Exiled CEO):**
> "Older Hispanic female, sharp cheekbones, graying hair pulled back tightly. Wearing battered desert-punk combat armor with twilight purple accents. Carries a sawed-off shotgun. Grinning with blood-stained teeth."

---

## 4. EXAMPLE FULL PROMPT
*If you want to generate a scene of Kalani fighting a Health Inspector in a Grill House:*

> "3D painted animation still, Fortiche Production style, Arcane animated series aesthetic. Thick visible oil brushstrokes over 3D geometry. Extreme rim lighting. Matte painting background. Deep chiaroscuro lighting, high contrast. Depressing 1997 Pacific Northwest industrial city. Glum, overcast, heavy continuous rain. Mid-rise brick buildings, rundown 1990s strip malls, dense dark pine trees shrouded in fog. Gritty, sweat-stained, industrial decay. Faded, unreadable light-box signs and peeling painted brick advertisements. GROUNDED 1997 REALITY ONLY. NO NEON GEOMETRIC SHAPES. NO SCI-FI. NO MEGA-STRUCTURES. NO REAL WORLD LOGOS. NO FAST FOOD BRANDS. NO READABLE TEXT. Lighting: Heat Lamp Glow. Oppressive, sweat-inducing amber and burnt orange. Deep brown shadows. SUBJECT: A massive Pacific Islander male wearing a heavy Kevlar butcher's apron and a hydraulic 'Meat-Tenderizer' gauntlet, furiously punching a faceless soldier wearing a bone-white hazmat plague-doctor suit. Toxic green gas filling the air."
# 🎨 ART CORE: THE MASTER PROMPT (V2.1 REALITY)
**Department:** Art & QA (Sedona)
**Date:** May 2, 2026

To ensure the "Franchise Underworld" universe maintains strict visual consistency and never bleeds into generic cyberpunk or comic book tropes, we have established this locked Master Prompt via the Gemini engine.

Every single image generated for this project MUST use this exact structural suffix.

## THE DEFINITIVE MASTER PROMPT
Whenever prompting Sedona or Midjourney, use this formula:

`[Primary Subject/Scene Description], highly detailed graphic novel illustration, thick oil painting texture, visible brushstrokes, painterly arcane-hybrid realism, warm moody lighting, 1997 analog labor realism noir style, deep chiaroscuro, desaturated but rich color palette, brutalist architecture, grease, decay, NO photorealism, NO camera lens effects, NO real-world logos or brands (e.g. no McDonald's), ABSOLUTELY NO TEXT, NO WORDS, NO LETTERS, NO CAPTIONS, authentic to 90s prestige indie comics, [Specific Panel/Concept Details]`

## EXAMPLE USAGE
*"Kalani Morn holding a meat hook in a freezing walk-in freezer. Shift-Light Green LED from a frozen thermometer glows in the fog. highly detailed graphic novel illustration, thick oil painting texture, visible brushstrokes, painterly arcane-hybrid realism, cold blue lighting, 1997 analog labor realism noir style, deep chiaroscuro, desaturated but rich color palette, brutalist architecture, grease, decay, NO photorealism, NO camera lens effects, NO real-world logos or brands, authentic to 90s prestige indie comics."*

## 💡 LIGHTING & COLOR MOTIFS
To unify the factions visually and emphasize the narrative themes of labor, all prompt descriptions must incorporate one of the following lighting motifs depending on the character's current state:

### 1. The "Shift-Light" Green (On-the-Clock)
When a character is mid-shift, working, or executing franchise operations, the scene must feature a **sickly, piercing green light source**. This represents the oppressive weight of the franchise and the anxiety of being "on the clock."
*   *Examples:* Green dashboard glow, green digital clock reflection, green industrial "Active" light, green status lights on machinery, green emergency exit sign.

### 2. The "Tobacco-Stained Yellow" (Off-Shift)
When a character is in a rare moment of privacy, resting, or off-shift in their own sanctuary, the scene must feature a **warm, heavy, tobacco-stained yellow light**. This represents fleeting safety, physical exhaustion, and the decay of their personal lives outside the franchise.
*   *Examples:* A flickering yellow incandescent bulb in a cheap apartment, the glow of a lit cigarette in a dark room, a yellow streetlamp bleeding through dirty blinds.

### 3. The "Heat-Lamp Red" (The Rush / Combat)
When a character is in the middle of extreme violence, an assassination, or the chaotic peak of a "lunch rush," the scene must be dominated by an aggressive **blinding, greasy heat-lamp red or deep fryer orange**. This represents the boiling point of the labor, adrenaline, and blood.
*   *Examples:* A shattered heat-lamp raining sparks, the glow of a raging grease fire, emergency flare lighting, the red-hot coils of an industrial toaster.

### 4. The "Halogen Bleach" (Corporate / Management)
When a character interacts with The Board, executives (like Caine), or is inside the untouchable corporate heights of Lumenridge, the light must be a **sterile, freezing halogen white or clinical pale blue**. It represents the sociopathy of the upper class—devoid of grease, warmth, or humanity. 
*   *Examples:* Unflickering fluorescent tubes reflecting off stainless steel, a glowing white laptop screen in a dark boardroom, sterile freezer lighting.

### 5. The "Pilot-Light Blue" (Termination / Death)
When a character is killed, "terminated" by the company, or completely broken by burnout, the scene should be consumed by deep, heavy shadows, illuminated only by a single **faint, cold pilot-light blue**. It represents the end of the shift, the extinguishing of the worker's utility.
*   *Examples:* A dying gas stove ring, the glow of a disconnected pager, moonlight reflecting off a puddle of spilled ghost oil.
# ZERO HALLUCINATION SAFEGUARDS

**Mandatory Negative Prompt String (Append to all AI image generation):**
`--no text, letters, words, signatures, watermarks, floating limbs, extra fingers, missing torso, modern brand logos, cartoon styling, cell shading`
# 🔬 Sedona's QA Checklist (Art Directives)

> **Art Director Note (Sedona):** No image becomes canon until it passes this checklist. If an image fails, it is sent back to the prompter (The Orchestrator) for refinement or inpainting.

## 1. ARCHITECTURAL LOGIC
- [ ] **Light Sources:** Is there a single, logical light source? (No random glowing objects unless justified by the lore).
- [ ] **Infrastructure:** Are streetlamps spaced realistically (e.g., every 50-70 feet)?
- [ ] **Industry:** Are smoke stacks ONLY present in the Industrial Grid? (Ensure they aren't pasted onto residential buildings).
- [ ] **Shadows:** Do the cast shadows match the angle of the primary light source?

## 2. THEMATIC CONSISTENCY (The "Underworld" Vibe)
- [ ] **Grounded Noir:** Does this feel like a gritty, late-90s conspiracy thriller?
- [ ] **Service-Industry Focus:** Are the elements of work, struggle, and corporate dominance visible?
- [ ] **Color Palette:** Does the image adhere to the specific Faction's color rules? (e.g., Neon Cartel = Cyan/Magenta, The 24s = Fluorescent White/Yellow).

## 3. HALLUCINATION CHECK
- [ ] **Text/Signage:** Is any background text scrambled or nonsensical? (If yes, inpaint or blur it).
- [ ] **Anatomy:** Do the characters have the correct number of fingers/limbs? 
- [ ] **Physics:** Is rain falling straight down or at a consistent angle? (No intersecting rain patterns).

---
*If all boxes are checked, the image is approved and added to the Visual Bible.*
# FRANCHISE UNDERWORLD: VISUAL BIBLE - DISTRICTS

**Aesthetic Target:** 1997 Pacific Northwest Industrial. 3D Painted Animation Still (Fortiche/Arcane style). Heavy rain, overcast, gloomy. No sci-fi mega-structures.

---

## 1. DOWNTOWN: Citadel Ridge
**The Corporate Hub:** Towering brutalist concrete mid-rises piercing the cloud layer. Clean, sterile, and heavily militarized. The only place in the city that sees natural sunlight, though often obscured by rain. Lit by cold blue and clinical white lighting.

![Citadel Ridge](/Users/foxtrot1/GABAnode-Portfolio/01-CORE-PRODUCT/Franchise-Underworld/PRODUCTION_SPRINTS/assets/district_citadel_ridge_1777879109828.png)

---

## 2. HIGH-DENSITY URBAN: Blackpole Commons
**The Labor Slums:** Stacked concrete tenements, claustrophobic brick alleys, and rusting metal catwalks. Perpetual twilight caused by the smog layer. The streets are lit by faded, unreadable light-box signs and peeling painted brick advertisements. 

![Blackpole Commons](/Users/foxtrot1/GABAnode-Portfolio/01-CORE-PRODUCT/Franchise-Underworld/PRODUCTION_SPRINTS/assets/district_blackpole_commons_1777879120658.png)

---

## 3. THE DOCKS: The Ghost Oil Refineries
**The Industrial Engine:** Massive smokestacks belching black smoke, hissing pipelines, and open vats of boiling synthetic oil located on the polluted waterfront. Almost entirely lit by the harsh amber glow of burning excess gas from the flare stacks.

![Ghost Oil Refineries](/Users/foxtrot1/GABAnode-Portfolio/01-CORE-PRODUCT/Franchise-Underworld/PRODUCTION_SPRINTS/assets/district_ghost_oil_refineries_1777879134049.png)

---

## 4. THE SUBURBS: The Neon Sprawl
**The Commercial Zones:** Endless 1990s strip malls, massive paved parking lots, and identical suburban tract housing for middle-management. Heavy, continuous rain washes out the grimy, flickering light-box signs. Depressing, repetitive suburban decay.

![The Neon Sprawl](/Users/foxtrot1/GABAnode-Portfolio/01-CORE-PRODUCT/Franchise-Underworld/PRODUCTION_SPRINTS/assets/district_neon_sprawl_1777879145834.png)

---

## 5. THE OUTSKIRTS: The Ash Flats
**The Wasteland:** A desolate expanse of dumped corporate waste surrounding the city limits. Endless, rain-slicked highways cutting through dense, dark, dying pine forests. The domain of the heavily armored Cartel food-trucks.

![The Ash Flats](/Users/foxtrot1/GABAnode-Portfolio/01-CORE-PRODUCT/Franchise-Underworld/PRODUCTION_SPRINTS/assets/district_ash_flats_1777879159629.png)
# 👁️ VISUAL BIBLE MASTER (V2.1)
**Department:** Art Direction (Sedona) & Archives (Octavia)
**Status:** CANON LOCKED

## I. GLOBAL VISUAL CANON & RENDER RULES ("Arcane Rendering Addendum")
These are the strict negative and positive constraints for all image generation (Midjourney, Gemini, Nanobanano, etc.).

*   **Rendering Style:** Painterly, Arcane-hybrid realism. 3D volumes with 2D hand-painted textures, visible brush strokes, and angular facial planes (Asaro-head anatomy). No glossy 3D or airbrushed skin.
*   **Tech Era Lock (1997–2005):** Strictly analog. 
    *   *Allowed:* Nokia candy-bar phones, Motorola Razrs, pagers, CRT monitors, fax machines, payphones. 
    *   *Forbidden:* Smartphones, modern LEDs, touchscreens, futuristic UI, cyberpunk aesthetics.
*   **Color Philosophy:** Shifted and desaturated franchise-inspired palettes. No literal brand colors. Shadows must be colorized (violet, teal, deep magenta), never pure black.

## II. THE 5 LIGHTING MODES
If lighting is not one of these 5, it is a hallucination and must be inpainted.
1.  **Cold Rain Noir:** Deep blues, wet reflections.
2.  **Sodium Fog:** Amber streetlights cutting through haze.
3.  **Neon Gloom:** Muted purple/pink on wet asphalt (reflections only, never bright glowing signs).
4.  **Diner Glow:** Warm yellow interior light.
5.  **CRT Glow:** Greenish analog screen reflections.

## III. DIVERSITY & PHENOTYPE CASTING LOCKS
To prevent AI from defaulting to generic Eurocentric features, use these strict casting locks:

*   **Julian Pike:** Afro-Latino (Dominican/Puerto Rican + White). Medium olive-brown skin, rain-damp dark curls, deep-set tired eyes.
*   **Pastor Jonah Creed:** Black (African American). Warm chestnut skin, gray curls, broad features, weathered compassionate face.
*   **Kalani Morn (formerly Calder):** Pacific Islander (Samoan/Hawaiian). Deep warm-brown skin, heavy jaw, thick black hair, stoic resolve.
*   **Rhea Solomon:** Black (African American/Afro-Caribbean). Deep warm-brown skin, natural coils/puff, strong cheekbones, defiant expression.
*   **Delilah Strave:** Afro-Latina (light-skinned). Golden-brown skin, sleek styling, poised, calculated charm.
*   **Elara Holt:** Asian American (Filipina + White mixed). Neutral beige skin, almond eyes, straight dark hair, analytical stillness.
*   **Marcus Vallen Sr.:** Mediterranean White (Italian/Greek). Olive skin, silvered hair, sharp Roman nose, corporate menace.
*   **Marcus Vallen Jr.:** Mediterranean White. Lighter olive skin, flushed/stressed complexion, anxious bravado.
*   **Luca Ramos:** Latino (Mexican-American). Golden-brown skin, messy curls, youthful hopeful fear.

## IV. SYMBOL SYSTEMS
All symbols must be geometric, abstract, and diegetic (appearing as chalk, stamps, or etchings). No literal corporate logos.
*   **The Burning Crown (Primary):** Broken circular ring with three gaps (fractured halo) and a downward-pointing interior wedge (flame cut). Muted gold/tarnished brass.
*   **The Table of Ten (Secondary):** A decagon with three hairline fractures and a diagonal slash. Muted iron/aged silver.
